channel.title: The Dark Lantern
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Dark Lantern" - A creature that turns you into a tree.

item.title: Re: The Dark Lantern
item.content:encoded:
[+] Open for crit
– hide block

So, I was thinking that for the story, someone would find a journal of the creature in that dimension and based on the notes there he would discover that the creature was once a human but it was transformed into this anomaly after having found this lantern. He became the lantern bearer and was corrupted by it.

My personal feeling, and other reviewers may disagree, is that backstory like this is almost always the wrong approach to take in writing a SCP: the core issue (for me) is that the Foundation are writing this article, and so it's easier to get a reader invested if the Foundation actively take a protagonistic role in the story (i.e., they are the PoV telling the story, their decisions are the ones that should actually affect it). I don't necessarily dislike this as a start but imo you should take this and run with it and explore how the SCP feels know their the lantern bearer and how that's affected by the Foundation coming into view.

Uhhh, I forgot to mention it’s an oil lantern. It needs oil to stay lit, not wood. When it transforms you, you’ll become a tree full of oil. Then it will use that oil to fuel the lantern.

I'm a little torn on this because on the one hand, it still feels very complex but on the other, I guess it's kinda how rubber trees work.

I was thinking that it would add some nice background information, you know, make things more realistic, more believable.

Sure but you're trying to tell a cohesive short story here, adding in stuff that adds to the realism isn't necessarily a good idea if it doesn't add to the core of the story being told.

item.title: Re: The Dark Lantern
item.content:encoded:
My concern is you don't really have a cohesive story around this so much as a weird DnD monster; and if you don't have the story down, this won't work.

So, I was thinking that for the story, someone would find a journal of the creature in that dimension and based on the notes there he would discover that the creature was once a human but it was transformed into this anomaly after having found this lantern. He became the lantern bearer and was corrupted by it.

why does this thing bother turning humans into wood in order to burn them in it's lantern instead of like, just using regular wood when it's in a forest?

Uhhh, I forgot to mention it’s an oil lantern. It needs oil to stay lit, not wood. When it transforms you, you’ll become a tree full of oil. Then it will use that oil to fuel the lantern. There is not source for oil in that dimension other than this.

What does the history of its manifestations add?

I was thinking that it would add some nice background information, you know, make things more realistic, more believable.

item.title: Re: The Dark Lantern
item.content:encoded:
[+] Open for crit
– hide block

Interviews with the creature (it is sapient and capable of speech) and history of its manifestations. It has been around for a long time.

So like, this is going to be the bulk of your narrative but like…what happens here? What story do the interviews tell? What does the history of it's manifestations add?

My concern is you don't really have a cohesive story around this so much as a weird DnD monster; and if you don't have the story down, this won't work.

The actual core anomaly feels…fine but a touch overcomplex perhaps: it has a lot of elements to it that feel a little like they don't necessarily add much; but without having a better idea of what you want to do with the article, I can't tell whether this overcomplexity matters. I guess at it's core my question is: why does this thing bother turning humans into wood in order to burn them in it's lantern instead of like, just using regular wood when it's in a forest? Like obviously narratively, that's more interesting; but the anomaly feels like it needs a motivation for this, y'know?

IMO: just have folk be tempted by a weird song they hear in the woods that makes them go 'wtf is that' and investigate; you don't need to like, making it so that they're forced to be affected by it (at least, that's what I'm assuming the cognitohazard does here)

item.title: Re: The Dark Lantern
item.content:encoded:
Hello, so here are some of my explanations. Hope this makes everything more clear ;)

Where does the entity come from?

It comes from another dimension that co-exists with our own. It has been in that dimension for an eternity and it occasionally visits our reality to lure more individuals into its pocket dimension.

Why does it need human wood to light its lantern?

The lantern needs oil to stay lit. There is no source for oil in its original dimension. So, when it transforms you, you will become a tree of oil which it will use to keep the lantern lit. It takes parts of the wood that contain oil and puts it in the lantern.

Also, what is the reason that phones and compasses wont work after a person is lost? Are they in a new pocket dimension?

When you hear the song, you will be lured away from the path. Then, when you get closer to it, you will transported into a pocket dimension which resembles a forest in autumn. There, electronic devices will be mostly useless. You can exit the dimension if you don’t lose hope. If you do, you will become a tree in that dimension.

item.title: Re: The Dark Lantern
item.content:encoded:
First off, I do like this idea, however, simply leading people off a path to get them lost sounds allot like a Will-o'-the-wisp.

I think you need to develop this a bit more to make it unique.
Where does the entity come from?
Why does it need human wood to light its lantern?

Also, what is the reason that phones and compasses wont work after a person is lost?
Is there some anomaly created that effects these?
Are they in a new pocket dimension? etc,

TLDR: Needs more work to be unique, then a bit more work to make it more interesting.

item.title: The Dark Lantern
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: Yes

Page Type: SCP Article

Genre (Optional): Action, History

Elevator Pitch: A tall humanoid creature that has 2 luminous white eyes and 2 horn-like appendages resembling those of the Cervidae family. A small clothing piece seems to be covering the creature's upper part of the body and it has also been seen carrying a lantern with it. This creature manifests in any remote forested area in which humans pass. Once it manifests, it will then attempt to separate individuals from each other (if the individual is not already alone) by singing a song . The song is cognitohazardous, but it has a short range. Each individual hearing it will be tempted to follow the source of the song until it stops. Once that happens, they will be lost in the forest in which they are located with no knowledge of how they got there or how to go back. From this stage, most people make it back to society. Those who don't (cell phones and compasses won’t work) will begin to experience fatigue and illness after 1-2 days from being lost. After that, individuals will be incapable of walking or staying conscious. In the last stage, roots will begin to grow from and around these individuals. They will also begin to cough leaves as they grow inside them. Branches will also grow from their body. Slowly, they will be completely covered in wood until their original body becomes a tree. After that, SCP-XXXX will approach the tree and cut it down. It will then pick up some of the wood and put it in its lantern to keep it lit.

Central Narrative: Interviews with the creature (it is sapient and capable of speech) and history of its manifestations. It has been around for a long time.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: The creature manifestations appear in a very specific part of the year, October 31th up to November 31th. It is theorized that it can teleport, but this
hasn't been confirmed. (Spoiler) The lantern that it carries contains its soul, it must keep it lit in order to survive. That’s why it needs wood. Normal wood doesn’t seem to work in keeping it lit.

Additional Notes: I don't really have anything to say, but I hope you like the concept. I can explain the idea in more detail if there are some things that are unclear!
